back_translation: After Abimelech, Tola son of Puah son of Dodo, a man of Issachar, arose to save Israel. He dwelt in Shamir in the hill country of Ephraim.
Tola judged Israel for twenty-three years, and he died and was buried in Shamir.
After him arose Jair the Gileadite, and he judged Israel for twenty-two years.
He had thirty sons who rode on thirty donkeys, and they had thirty cities, which to this day are called Havvoth-jair, in the land of Gilead.
Jair died and was buried in Kamon.

The descendants of Israel again did evil in the sight of the LORD. They served the Baals, the Ashtoreths, the gods of Aram, the gods of Sidon, the gods of Moab, the gods of the descendants of Ammon, and the gods of the Philistines. They forsook the LORD and did not serve Him.
The anger of the LORD burned against Israel, and He sold them into the hand of the Philistines and into the hand of the descendants of Ammon.

They crushed and oppressed the descendants of Israel in that year. For eighteen years they oppressed all the descendants of Israel who were on the other side of the Jordan in the land of the Amorites in Gilead.
The descendants of Ammon also crossed the Jordan to fight against Judah, Benjamin, and the house of Ephraim, and Israel was in great distress.
The descendants of Israel cried out to the LORD, saying, 'We have sinned against You, because we have forsaken our God and have served the Baals.'
The LORD said to the descendants of Israel, 'Was it not from Egypt, from the Amorites, from the descendants of Ammon, and from the Philistines —'
'and the Sidonians, Amalek, and Maon oppressed you, and you cried out to Me, and I saved you from their hand?'
'But you have forsaken Me and have served other gods. Therefore I will not save you again.'
'Go and cry out to the gods you have chosen. Let them save you in the time of your distress.'
The descendants of Israel said to the LORD, 'We have sinned. Do to us as is good in Your sight, but please rescue us this day.'
They put away the foreign gods from among them and served the LORD, and His soul could no longer bear the misery of Israel.
The descendants of Ammon were called out and encamped in Gilead, and the descendants of Israel gathered and encamped at Mizpah.
The leaders of Gilead said to one another, 'Who is the man who will begin to fight against the descendants of Ammon? He shall be head over all who dwell in Gilead.'
